Accommodation · Tonight (arriving Hiroshima)

Sheraton Grand Hiroshima or Hotel Granvia Hiroshima — both are directly connected to Hiroshima Station, large enough for 6, and well-positioned for the Peace Park (15 min tram or 5 min taxi). Your Yamato luggage forwarded from Kyoto should have arrived by now.

Itinerary Map

Ise — Key Sites

Geku and Naiku — the two shrine precincts — connected by the old pilgrimage road through Ise town.

Where to Eat

Ise — Restaurants

Essential
Iseya Honten
Near Naiku · Ise Udon · ¥800–1,500

The definitive Ise udon restaurant. Thick, impossibly soft noodles in a sweet soy tare that has no equivalent in Japanese cuisine. Only made in Ise. This is a once-in-a-trip flavor. Eat here.

Essential
Akafuku Mochi Main Shop
Okage Yokocho · Mochi · ¥500–800

Made fresh, sold same-day only. Soft mochi with smooth sweet bean paste. The most historically significant sweet in Ise — sold continuously at this location since 1707. Buy and eat immediately.

Inn
Hoshidekan
Near Naiku · Traditional Inn · Breakfast included

Long-running family inn 5 min from Naiku. The breakfast is a proper Japanese set — grilled fish, miso, rice, pickles, seasonal small dishes. A grounding start to the final sacred day.

広島
July 26 · One Day

Hiroshima

One of the most morally serious destinations in the world. The city rebuilt on the ground where it was destroyed — itself a form of testament.

Day-by-Day
Map
Restaurants

Sunday, July 26 — Peace Park & Miyajima

Day 18
Morning · Peace Memorial Museum (Arrive at Opening)

The Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum documents the morning of August 6, 1945 with extraordinary precision — the human scale, the immediate aftermath, and the decades of radiation illness that followed. The 2019 renovation placed individual human objects at the center: a child's lunch box, a tricycle, a school uniform, a shadow burned into stone. Allow 2–3 hours. Entry ¥200. See official museum site for current exhibition details, and World History Encyclopedia on Hiroshima for historical context. For pre-reading with younger travelers: The Sadako Legacy Foundation.

The Atomic Bomb Dome (Genbaku Dome) — the skeletal remains of the Hiroshima Prefectural Industrial Promotion Hall, left standing as a memorial. It was directly beneath the detonation point and structurally survived through accident. The contrast between this ruin and the rebuilt, prosperous city is one of the most morally significant visual experiences in Japan.

Hiroshima — August 6, 1945At 8:15 AM, the atomic bomb "Little Boy" (uranium gun-type, ~15 kilotons) was detonated at 600 meters altitude. Immediately killed: estimated 70,000–80,000 people. By year-end: 90,000–166,000. The city was rebuilt on the same ground. The Peace Memorial Park occupies the hypocenter area. For the scientific, historical, and ethical dimensions, see Atomic Archive on Hiroshima and the Radiation Effects Research Foundation for the ongoing epidemiological study of survivors (hibakusha).
Afternoon · Miyajima Island (Optional)

25-minute ferry. The "floating" torii gate of Itsukushima Shrine is one of Japan's Three Views (Nihon Sankei). The shrine is built on stilts over the water — architectural submission to the tidal cycle. UNESCO World Heritage. See Encyclopedia of Shinto for the ritual significance of Itsukushima. Also: deer roam freely on the island and have been sacred messengers here for centuries.

Accommodation · Tonight

Sheraton Grand Hiroshima or Hotel Granvia Hiroshima — directly connected to Hiroshima Station. One night. Tomorrow morning: Nozomi shinkansen Hiroshima → Tokyo (~3h40m, ~¥17,000/person) — depart 8–9 AM to arrive Tokyo by midday.

📦 Tonight — Ship Bags to Tokyo Shinjuku (Yamato Transport)
The hotel front desk can arrange Yamato pickup directly — ask at check-in. Address bags to the Mic House Shinjuku Airbnb (1-17-8 Nishiochiai, Shinjuku-ku — write in Japanese). Request pickup for tonight before 9 PM; bags should arrive at the Shinjuku Airbnb the following day.
  • Hotel front desks handle Yamato routinely and often have delivery slips — easier than finding a 7-Eleven in an unfamiliar city
  • If the hotel can't arrange pickup, the 7-Eleven nearest to Hiroshima Station works — drop before 6–7 PM
  • Travel tomorrow on the shinkansen with daypacks only · Bags will be at the Airbnb when you arrive
✈️ Gupta Parents — Flying Home Today from Narita · July 26
While we're in Hiroshima, the Gupta parents fly home from Tokyo Narita (NRT) at 17:25 on Air Canada AC010 — the same flight number Asha and Sahana took on July 22, four days earlier.
AC010 — NRT 17:25 → Toronto (YYZ) 16:30 · Boeing 787-9 · 12h 5min · Economy Class
They will have traveled separately in Japan July 22–26 after the girls flew home. Full Gupta trip: July 13–26 in Japan.
Itinerary Map

Hiroshima — Key Sites

The Peace Memorial Park area, Atomic Bomb Dome, and Miyajima Island.

Where to Eat

Hiroshima — Restaurants

Essential Local
Okonomimura
Naka-ku · Hiroshima Okonomiyaki · ¥1,200–2,000

A building with 25 individual okonomiyaki restaurants across multiple floors — each chef's version slightly different. Hiroshima-style (layered, not mixed) is distinct from Osaka-style. Pick a floor by queue length. The whole building is the experience.

Historic
Micchan
Near Peace Park · Hiroshima Okonomiyaki · ¥1,200–1,800

Credited with creating the definitive Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki. Small, counter-only, always busy. The combination with kakiage (oyster) is the local standard. Worth any queue.

Oysters
Kanawa
Hiroshima Port · Oysters · ¥3,000–8,000

Hiroshima produces 70% of Japan's oysters. Kanawa is a floating restaurant moored in the harbor — the freshest oysters in the city, prepared every way. Particularly good grilled with butter and soy.

東京
July 27–29 · Three Nights

Tokyo Final

The city takes on different character after three weeks in Japan. Two full days for shopping, final dinners, and one last morning before the 5 PM departure.

Day-by-Day
Map
Restaurants

Monday, July 27 — Hiroshima → Tokyo · Creative Day

Day 19
Morning · Nozomi Shinkansen — Hiroshima → Tokyo

Depart Hiroshima Station on the Nozomi shinkansen at 8–9 AM (~3h40m, ~¥17,000/person, ~¥102,000 for six). Arrive Tokyo Station by noon. Take the Yamanote Line to Takadanobaba or Shinjuku, then to the Mic House Shinjuku Airbnb (Nishiochiai). Drop bags, recharge, and head out — three full days left.

Afternoon · Daikanyama & Nakameguro

Tsutaya T-Site (Daikanyama) — architecture as experience. A curated bookstore-campus for notebooks, design books, magazines, vinyl, and high-end stationery. Walk 10 minutes along the Meguro River canal to Nakameguro for independent boutiques and specialty coffee.

Evening · Omotesando, Harajuku & Shopping

Momotaro Jeans Aoyama — Japanese denim flagship. Free same-day hemming on antique chain-stitch machine. Traveler's Factory Omotesando — the best notebook destination in Japan (handcrafted buffalo leather, modular inserts). Itoya Ginza — 12 floors of Japanese stationery. Gundam Base Tokyo (Odaiba) or Kotobukiya Akihabara for model kits. Atmos Harajuku and Onitsuka Tiger Harajuku for Japan-exclusive sneakers.

Momotaro Jeans · AoyamaTraveler's Factory · NotebooksItoya · Ginza · 12 floorsGundam Base · Odaiba
Accommodation · July 27–29

Mic House Shinjuku Airbnb — 1-17-8 Nishiochiai, Shinjuku-ku. Three final nights. On July 29 (departure day), N'EX from Shinjuku to Narita Terminal 1 takes ~80 min — plan to leave the apartment by 1:30–2:00 PM for a 5 PM flight.

Tuesday, July 28 — Last Full Day

Day 20
During the Day · Hanging with Andrew Balmuth

Pam's friend Andrew Balmuth is in Tokyo — July 28 is the plan to spend time together. Andrew is a good Tokyo guide: coordinate the day around where he wants to take us, whether that's a neighborhood walk, a lunch spot he loves, or just catching up over coffee. Flexible day — build around Andrew's availability.

Afternoon · teamLab Borderless or Akihabara

teamLab Borderless Tokyo (Azabudai Hills) — 50+ interconnected digital artworks in spaces where boundaries dissolve. Tickets ¥3,200 adults, book at teamlab.art. 2–4 hours. Extraordinary for children and adults equally. Alternative: full Akihabara run for final Gundam, anime, and electronics purchases.

Evening · Final Dinner

Tamawarai (Bib Gourmand soba, Omotesando) or Tempura Kondo (Michelin 2★, Ginza) — both are appropriate final dinners that capture something essentially Japanese. If Andrew joins for dinner, he'll know good options.

Wednesday, July 29 — Departure

Day 21
Morning · Final Hours in Tokyo

Departure at 5 PM means a genuinely leisurely morning. A last coffee walk — Nakameguro canal or Shimokitazawa's vintage shops. A final bowl of ramen at a neighborhood place that has no rating and needs none. Checkout from the Shinjuku Airbnb, leave bags by the door.

Afternoon · Narita Airport — UA838 to San Francisco

Leave the apartment by 12:45 PM. Take the Narita Express (N'EX) from Shinjuku Station directly to Narita Terminal 1 (~80 min, ¥3,070/person). Arrive at Narita by ~2:05 PM — 3 hours before the 5:05 PM departure. For a family of 5 with international check-in, security, and immigration, 3 hours is right.

UA838 · Depart Narita (NRT) 5:05 PM → Arrive San Francisco (SFO) 10:40 AM (same day — you gain back the day you lost crossing the Pacific). ~9h35m flight.

UA649 · Depart San Francisco (SFO) 9:13 PM → Arrive Tampa (TPA) Thursday July 30, 5:38 AM. Long layover at SFO (~10h33m) — dinner, walk the terminal, rest.

At Narita before boarding: the duty-free run is essential. Best picks: Japanese Kit Kat (matcha, sake, regional flavors — buy in bulk), Lupicia hojicha tea, and Umeboshi plums. The international departures terminal at NRT T1 has good food options airside as well.

Itinerary Map

Tokyo Final — Key Sites

Shopping, final dining, and departure logistics across central and west Tokyo.

Where to Eat

Tokyo Final — Restaurants

Michelin 2★
Tempura Kondo
Ginza · Tempura · ¥15,000–25,000

The finest tempura in Tokyo — a worthy last night. Chef Kondo's seasonal vegetable courses redefine what tempura is capable of. Reserve 1–2 months ahead.

Bib Gourmand
Tamawarai
Omotesando · Soba · ¥2,000–3,500

Home-grown buckwheat, milled in-house. The freshest, most refined soba in Tokyo. Appropriate final dinner for a trip that began with kaiseki.

Bib Gourmand
Negima
Shinjuku · Izakaya · ¥3,000–5,000

8 seats. Traditional kimono. Time-capsule atmosphere. Cash only. The kind of place that makes you want to come back to Japan immediately.

Final Ramen
Fuunji
Shinjuku · Tsukemen · ¥1,200

One of Tokyo's best tsukemen (dipping ramen). Concentrated chicken tare, thick noodles. The right final bowl on the morning of departure. Open early.

Finance

Daily Budget — 6 People

Estimates in USD. July is peak season. These ranges reflect comfortable family travel — not budget backpacking, not luxury. The JR Pass recommendation has been corrected from the original guide: the current 21-day price is ¥100,000/person (~$665), not ¥70,000. Individual tickets for your specific route total ~¥72,000/person, making the pass a net loss. See Before You Go for the detailed breakdown.

CategoryPer Night / DayNotesTotal (21 days)
Accommodation
Tokyo 6 nights, Kyoto 6 nights, Hokkaido 3 nights, Kumano 3 nights, Ise 1 night, Hiroshima 1 night		$280–500/night avg. $350Airbnb (machiya in Kyoto), minshuku at Kumano (meals included), ryokan 1 night Hokkaido, hotels Ise/Hiroshima.$7,500–9,000
Individual Shinkansen Tickets
Replaces JR Pass. All inter-city rail, point-to-point via SmartEX		~¥72,000/personTokyo↔Kyoto x2, Kyoto→Kii-Tanabe, Kumano→Nagoya, Nagoya→Hiroshima, Hiroshima→Tokyo. ~$480/person at current exchange rate.$2,880 (6 adults)
Hokkaido Flights (roundtrip)~$120/person rtANA/JAL HND↔CTS. Book early for July peak pricing.$720
Local Transit (Suica)
Tokyo Metro, Kyoto buses, taxis, Hakone		$15–30/person/dayLoad ¥5,000–8,000 per Suica card. Tokyo subway ~¥200/ride. Kyoto buses ¥230 flat fare.$1,800–2,500
Meals
Breakfast, lunch, dinner — including 4 special dinners		$50–80/person/day avgMix of Bib Gourmand, neighborhood spots, konbini breakfast, 1–2 Michelin dinners. Kumano meals included in accommodation.$10,500–14,000
Museums & Entry Tickets$10–25/person/dayGhibli ¥1,000, Nintendo ¥3,300, TNM ¥1,000, Peace Museum ¥200, temple entries ¥400–1,000 each, Hakone ¥1,600, teamLab ¥3,200.$1,500–2,000
Car Rental (Hokkaido, 2 days)~$90/day 7-seaterFor Furano/Biei access. Book from international site before arrival.$180
Shopping (variable)—Denim ¥20,000–60,000/pair. Premium notebooks ¥2,000–8,000. Gundam kits ¥3,000–30,000+. Sneakers ¥10,000–30,000. Highly personal.$1,000–5,000+
Estimated Total (6 People, 21 Days)
Excluding international flights and personal shopping		$26,000–36,000
≈ $4,300–6,000/person
Where to Stay

Accommodation Guide

For six people, an Airbnb apartment is almost always more economical and comfortable than two hotel rooms. The Kyoto machiya experience is non-negotiable — it is part of the cultural substance of the trip.

Tokyo

July 9, 14–16, 27–29 · Six Nights

Best neighborhoods: Shinjuku (most central), Nakameguro/Daikanyama (stylish, walkable), Aoyama (upscale, quiet). Search Airbnb for "3LDK Tokyo 6 guests" with washer/dryer, elevator, under 10 min walk to station.

$180–320 /night for 6

Kyoto — Machiya

July 10–13 & 20–21 · Six Nights

Book a machiya townhouse. Best sources: kyoto-machiya-inn.com (specialist company, licensed properties, several sleep 6 exactly — 'Kiyu', 'Natsume-an', 'Kohaku-an') and Airbnb (search "Kyoto machiya 6 guests Higashiyama"). Book now — July fills fast.

$250–400 /night for 6

Hokkaido (Regular)

July 17 & 19 · Two Nights

Sapporo Nakajima Park area apartment — 2-bedroom, 6 guests, 5 min walk to subway. Multiple listings on Airbnb near Susukino/Nakajima Park. Alternative: base in Furano for closer Biei access.

$150–240 /night for 6

Hokkaido Ryokan ★

July 18 · One Night · Dinner + Breakfast Included

Zaborin (Niseko) — Japan's most acclaimed design ryokan. Individual villas, private outdoor rotenburo, architecture by Jun Igarashi. Book 4–6 months ahead directly at zaborin.com.
Jozankei Tsuruga Bettei Mori no Uta — more accessible (40 min from Sapporo), private baths, excellent kaiseki, family-friendly.

$700–1,500 /night (dinner + breakfast included)

Kumano Kodo — Minshuku

July 22–24 · Three Nights · Meals Included

Book via tb-kumano.jp/en (official Tanabe City Tourism Bureau). Request Nakahechi route with nights at Chikatsuyu-Oji, Nonaka, and Koguchi/Nachi. Email directly — they handle English group bookings. Book now for July.

$80–150 /person/night (dinner + breakfast)

Ise & Hiroshima

July 25–26 · One Night Each

Ise: Hoshidekan (hoshidekan.co.jp) — traditional inn, Japanese breakfast, 5 min from Naiku.
Hiroshima: Sheraton Grand or Hotel Granvia (both connected to Hiroshima Station, family rooms available).

$180–350 /night for 6
Acquisitions

Shopping Guide

Japan produces the finest versions of a remarkable number of things. This is a guide to finding them.

Aritsugu — Kyoto Knives

Founded 1560 · Nishiki Market · Craft Lineage

Aritsugu (有次) has supplied the imperial kitchen and professional chefs for 450+ years. Hand-forged carbon-steel knives in all traditional Japanese styles (yanagiba, deba, usuba), copper cookware, bamboo tools. The shop will engrave your name in Japanese characters on the blade. A direct connection to one of Kyoto's deepest artisanal lineages.

Kappabashi Kitchen Town

Tokyo · 170+ Shops · Near Sensoji

Tokyo's professional kitchen supply district. Landmark knife shop: Kama-asa (est. 1908) — hand-forged carbon-steel blades, excellent English service. Also: copper pots, donabe clay pots, lacquerware, ceramics, and sampuru plastic food display models (a specifically Japanese commercial form, developed in the 1920s). Where Tokyo's professional restaurants buy their equipment.

Japanese Denim

Selvedge · Heritage Craft

Momotaro Jeans Aoyama — flagship, Zimbabwean cotton, pink selvedge, free same-day antique chain-stitch hemming. ¥20,000–60,000/pair.
Studio D'Artisan — one of the original "Osaka Five." Their cinchback jeans (15oz right-twill selvedge, deep indigo, high rise, looser fit) reference 1940s American workwear directly. One of the most historically grounded brands in the field.
Burgus Plus — natural indigo dyeing. The 1928 cinchback jeans and "Second Type" denim jacket (modeled on 1950s designs) are particularly recommended. Available at Hinoya Ueno.
Beams (Harajuku/Omotesando) — Japan's most influential multi-brand retailer. Their house-label denim includes oversized shirts with archival Japanese textile prints. More fashion-forward than pure selvedge but still craft-conscious.
Hinoya Ueno (Ameyoko) — multi-brand: Big John, Momotaro, Oni Denim, Burgus Plus, Studio D'Artisan. Best for comparison shopping.
Kapital Ebisu — art-meets-craft, premium selvage, Kojima heritage.
Bears Shimokitazawa — TCB Jeans, Oni, Full Count, collectors' market.

Notebooks & Stationery

Paper Craft · Analog Writing

Itoya Ginza — 12 floors, est. 1904. Hobonichi, Midori, Stalogy, Rhodia Japan editions, fountain pens, washi tape.
Traveler's Factory Omotesando — handstitched buffalo leather notebooks, seasonal exclusive colorways.
Tsutaya T-Site Daikanyama — art/design books, premium stationery.
Maruzen Nihonbashi — Japan's oldest Western bookseller.

Gundam & Model Kits

Plastic Models · Collectibles

Gundam Base Tokyo (Diver City, Odaiba) — Bandai flagship. Every grade; Tokyo-exclusive kits. Life-size Unicorn Gundam outside.
Kotobukiya Akihabara — Frame Arms, Hexa Gear, Japan-exclusive kits, paints, tools.
Yellow Submarine Akihabara — good inventory, competitive prices.

Japanese Sneakers

Footwear · Design

Onitsuka Tiger Harajuku — Japan-exclusive colorways of Mexico 66, Tiger Corsair. ¥10,000–20,000.
Atmos Tokyo Harajuku — exclusive collabs, ASICS, New Balance Japan, Nike ACG.
ASICS Store Shinjuku — Japan-only models (Gel-Kayano Trainer, Gel-Lyte V), often 30% cheaper than US.
New Balance Japan Harajuku — Made in USA 990 and 1300JP exclusive colorways.

Puzzles & Games

Craft · Play · Gifts

Tokyu Hands Shibuya — Epoch, Beverly, Yanoman puzzles (superior quality vs European); massive craft/hobby section.
Loft Shibuya — art puzzles, Ghibli-licensed items.
Village Vanguard Shimokitazawa — unusual games, obscure art puzzles, toys.
Nintendo Store Tokyo (Shibuya Parco) — licensed Nintendo puzzles, exclusive Switch accessories.

Food & Edible Gifts

Carry Home

Depachika (Mitsukoshi Ginza, Takashimaya Shinjuku B2) — premium matcha, yuzu products, wagashi, hojicha/matcha Kit Kat, wasabi crackers, vacuum-sealed miso and soba.
Akafuku Mochi — from Ise only, buy at source.
Tsukiji — dried bonito, nori, professional Japanese kitchen knives (Masamoto, Korin).
Nishiki Market, Kyoto — yatsuhashi, Kyoto pickles, premium Uji matcha.

Prepare Your Mind

Reading List

Read before, during, and after. Organized by theme. The academic reading list is long — choose by where you are in the itinerary.

Zen & Buddhist Foundations

The Record of Rinzai (Rinzai-roku)

Linji Yixuan, c. 9th century · Read at The Zen Site

The foundational text of Rinzai Zen — the tradition underlying Daitoku-ji, Tenryu-ji, Ryoan-ji, and Nanzen-ji. Linji's confrontational pedagogical style (the shout, the blow) becomes comprehensible in the garden at Ryoan-ji.

Shobogenzo (Treasury of the True Dharma Eye)

Eihei Dogen, 1231–1253 · Read at The Zen Site

The masterwork of Soto Zen — Dogen's "Mountains and Rivers Sutra" is essential reading before the Kumano walk. His treatment of landscape as Buddhist practice makes the pilgrimage intelligible as philosophy.

Japanese Zen

Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy · Read at Stanford

The most rigorous scholarly overview of Zen Buddhism in Japan — covers the Chinese transmission, Eisai and Dogen, the five mountain system, and the relationship between Zen and Japanese aesthetics.

Zen Art

The Zen Site · Read at The Zen Site

The visual grammar of Zen — brushwork, gardens, pottery, architecture — and the aesthetic concepts (wabi, sabi, ma, mu) that link them. Useful throughout the Kyoto visits.

Shinto & Kumano

Encyclopedia of Shinto

Kokugakuin University · Read Online

The most authoritative English-language reference on Shinto theology, ritual, and history. Search for Ise, Inari, Kumano, Amaterasu, and shinbutsu-shugo — all directly relevant to the itinerary.

Kumano Kodo: Pilgrimage to Powerspots

J. Christian Greer & Michelle K. Oing, 2022

Part scholarly history, part travelogue, part speculative fiction. Covers Kumano folklore, ghost stories, and the nature of pilgrimage itself with rigor and humor. Essential pre-reading for the walk.

Japan's Kumano Kodo Pilgrimage

Kat Davis (Cicerone Guide) · Official history at Tanabe City

The practical trail guide with full route descriptions, accommodation lists, and cultural notes. Buy in physical form. Also: the UNESCO listing at whc.unesco.org.

Literature & Aesthetics

The Temple of the Golden Pavilion

Yukio Mishima, 1956

Essential before Kinkakuji. The obsessive inner world of the young monk who burns the temple. Japan's greatest modern novel and a profound meditation on beauty, destruction, and the sacred. Read on the shinkansen to Kyoto.

In Praise of Shadows

Jun'ichirō Tanizaki, 1933

A short essay on Japanese aesthetics — beauty of darkness, lacquer, shadow, restraint. After Daitoku-ji and the kaiseki dinner sequence, this explains why everything beautiful in Japan looks the way it does.

The Narrow Road to the Deep North

Matsuo Bashō, 1702 · Read at Sacred Texts

Bashō's travel journal through northern Japan. The aesthetic sensibility — attention, impermanence, seasonal beauty — is the backbone of everything you'll see in Japan. Short and transportive.

A Tale for the Time Being

Ruth Ozeki, 2013

Spans Tokyo and the Pacific Northwest, weaving Zen Buddhism, teenage diaries, and questions of time and catastrophe. Booker Prize shortlisted. Best read on the long train rides during the trip.

Food Culture

Rice, Noodle, Fish

Matt Goulding, 2015

The best English-language book on Japanese food culture — organized by ingredient and region. Covers ramen, sushi, yakitori, and kaiseki with equal depth. Read before the trip.

Kaiseki: The Exquisite Cuisine of Kyoto's Kikunoi Restaurant

Murata Yoshihiro, 2006

The definitive text on kaiseki cuisine by the chef whose restaurant anchors two nights of the Kyoto dining plan. Makes the kaiseki dinners fully intelligible as a cultural form.

For the Kids (Pre-Trip)

Sadako and the Thousand Paper Cranes

Eleanor Coerr, 1977 · The Sadako Legacy Foundation

Essential pre-reading for Hiroshima. Sadako Sasaki folded paper cranes until her death from leukemia caused by the bombing's radiation. Brief, moving, and the right preparation for the Peace Museum.

Studio Ghibli Films

Hayao Miyazaki / Studio Ghibli

Watch before the museum visit: My Neighbor Totoro, Spirited Away, Princess Mononoke, Howl's Moving Castle. Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind (manga, ages 12+) for the most sustained ecological and philosophical depth.

予定
Japan · July 9–29, 2026 · 6 Travelers

Booking & To-Do Checklist

Every reservation, ticket, permit, and task — in order of urgency.

0 of 0 items complete
Do This Now
Urgent — This Week
🚨

Nintendo Museum Lottery — Closes April 30

Today is April 5. You have 25 days to enter the lottery for Nintendo Museum tickets (July 11). Go to museum-tickets.nintendo.com — create a free Nintendo Account, then enter the lottery for up to 6 tickets. Bring passports on the day. This cannot be done after April 30.

🎮 Enter Nintendo Museum lottery (museum-tickets.nintendo.com)
April 1–30 only · Free Nintendo Account required · Up to 6 tickets · Bring passports on July 11
By Apr 30
🏡 Book Hokkaido Ryokan — Zaborin (Niseko) or Jozankei Tsuruga
July 18 · Dinner + breakfast included · Book 4–6 months ahead · zaborin.com or direct email · ~$700–1,500/night
ASAP
🥩 Book Kumano Minshuku accommodation (3 nights)
July 22–24 · tb-kumano.jp/en · Email for group bookings · Request Nakahechi route: Chikatsuyu → Nonaka → Koguchi/Nachi · July fills fast
ASAP
✈ Book domestic flight Tokyo (HND) → Sapporo (CTS)
July 17 morning · ANA or JAL · ~$100–150/person if booked now · Not JR Pass · Book 6 people together · ~$720 total
ASAP
🍽 Reserve Kikunoi Honten or Hyotei (Kyoto kaiseki dinner)
For July 10–13 or July 20–21 · 2–3 months ahead · Email directly or via Tableall · Hyotei: hyotei.co.jp · Kikunoi: kikunoi.jp
ASAP
🍽 Reserve Lurra° (Kyoto · Michelin 1★)
July 20–21 · Contemporary tasting menu · Very small room, high demand · Tableall or direct reservation
ASAP
🦀 Reserve Kani Honke (Hokkaido crab kaiseki)
July 17 evening, Sapporo · ¥8,000–20,000/person · Signature Hokkaido experience · Book now
ASAP
🚗 Book car rental in Hokkaido (7-seater, 2 days)
July 17–18 · Furano and Biei access · ~$90/day · Book from international site before arrival · International driving permit required
ASAP
Pre-Trip
Before Departure — By Date
By April 30
🎮 Nintendo Museum lottery (see Urgent above)
museum-tickets.nintendo.com · April 1–30
Apr 30
By May — Restaurants (2–3 months ahead)
🍽 Reserve Sézanne (Tokyo · Michelin 3★, Four Seasons Marunouchi)
July 14–16 or 27–28 · ¥30,000–50,000/person · Book through hotel concierge · Reserve well in advance
May
🍽 Reserve Tempura Kondo (Tokyo · Michelin 2★, Ginza)
For July 27–28 (Tokyo Final) · ¥15,000–25,000 · 1–2 months ahead
By May
🍽 Reserve Negima izakaya (Tokyo · Shinjuku · 8 seats)
For July 27 or 28 evenings · Cash only · Meiji atmosphere · Reserve well in advance · Tiny, fills immediately
By May
🍽 Reserve Monk Kyoto (omakase counter · if you can get in)
¥25,000–40,000 · "If you can get in" — try Tableall, Omakase app · No formal website · Worth the effort
Try now
June 10 — Exact Time Required
🎬 Buy Ghibli Museum tickets — June 10 at 10:00 AM JST (8 PM EDT June 9)
Via Lawson Ticket (l-tix.jp) · ¥1,000/adult · Passport numbers required at booking · Sells out in under 5 min · Set an alarm
Jun 10
Accommodation Still Needed
🏨 Book Ise accommodation — July 25 (1 night)
Hoshidekan recommended (hoshidekan.co.jp) — traditional inn, 5 min from Naiku, Japanese breakfast · Or: Ise City Hotel · ~$180–350 for 6
Soon
🏨 Book Hiroshima accommodation — July 26 (1 night)
Sheraton Grand Hiroshima or Hotel Granvia (both connected to Hiroshima Station, family-friendly) · Book family rooms
Soon
🏨 Book Hokkaido Sapporo apartment (2 nights: July 17 & 19)
Airbnb near Nakajima Park or Susukino · 2-bedroom, 6 guests · Furano area alternative for closer Biei access
Soon
Transport — Book via SmartEX App
📱 Download SmartEX app and register foreign credit card
smart-ex.jp/en — QR boarding, English interface, small discounts · Download now and set up before booking any shinkansen
App first
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Tokyo → Kyoto (July 10, morning)
Hikari or Nozomi · ~2h15m–2h40m · ~¥13,000/person · Book via SmartEX · Reserve 6 seats together
SmartEX
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Kyoto → Tokyo (July 14, morning)
Hikari · 2h45m · ~¥13,000/person · Book via SmartEX · Reserve 6 seats together
SmartEX
🚅 Book Odakyu Romancecar: Shinjuku → Hakone-Yumoto (July 16, 7 AM)
Odakyu Line · 85 min · ¥2,470/person · NOT JR Pass · Book via Odakyu app or odakyu.jp · Early departure essential for clear Fuji views
Odakyu app
🚅 Book JR from Kyoto → Kii-Tanabe (July 22)
JR Kinokuni Line via Osaka · ~2.5–3 hours · Check SmartEX (some sections may not be covered) · Then bus to Takijiri
SmartEX
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Nagoya → Hiroshima (July 25 afternoon)
From Ise → Nagoya (JR) then Nozomi/Hikari to Hiroshima · ~2h from Nagoya · Book in advance via SmartEX
SmartEX
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Hiroshima → Tokyo (July 27)
Nozomi · ~3h40m · ~¥17,000/person · Book via SmartEX · Return to Tokyo for final 2 shopping days
SmartEX
🚅 Book return Romancecar: Hakone-Yumoto → Shinjuku (July 16, evening)
Back from Hakone loop · Book round-trip on Odakyu app same day as outbound
Odakyu app
Museum Tickets
🎨 Buy teamLab Borderless tickets (Azabudai Hills)
July 28 · ¥3,200/person · teamlab.art · Book 1–2 months ahead · Evening visit best
1–2 months ahead
🏔 Buy Hakone Free Pass (optional — covers ropeway, boat, buses)
¥4,000/adult · Odakyu · Covers Hakone ropeway, Lake Ashi boat, most Hakone buses · Buy with Romancecar ticket or separately
With Romancecar
Logistics & Documents
🪪 Get International Driving Permit (for Hokkaido car rental)
Required for all 6 drivers who may drive · Available at AAA with valid US license · Processing takes 1–2 weeks
AAA
📱 Pre-order Japan eSIM or research pocket Wi-Fi
For 6 people: one shared pocket Wi-Fi (rent at airport) is most economical · eSIM alternative: Ubigi, Airalo, or Nomad · Activate before landing if using eSIM
Before departure
📱 Download essential apps: Google Maps (offline), Google Translate (camera), Navitime Japan, SmartEX, Tabelog
Download offline Tokyo + Kyoto maps in Google Maps before you leave · Google Translate camera mode is essential for menus
Before departure
🔌 Buy Type A power adapters if any devices have 3-prong plugs
Japan uses two-prong outlets only · 100V/50–60Hz · Charge speeds may be slightly slower than US
Before packing
💴 Notify your bank of Japan travel dates
Alert all cards being brought · Budget ~¥50,000–80,000/person in cash total · 7-Eleven ATMs most reliable for foreign cards
Before departure
📋 Save all accommodation names, addresses & phone numbers on phone
Needed for disembarkation and customs cards on the plane · Much easier to fill in mid-flight than to hunt through email at immigration
Before boarding
🎒 Arrange luggage strategy — plan Yamato Transport shipping days
Ship heavy bags between cities: ¥1,500–2,500/bag · Essential for Kumano (walk with daypacks only) · Hotels and konbini handle pickup · Plan city-to-city transfers
Plan ahead
👘 Confirm onsen tattoo policy at Hokkaido ryokan
Most traditional onsen prohibit tattoos — confirm policy before booking for any family members with visible tattoos · Some ryokan have private baths as an alternative
Check policy
Day of Arrival
At the Airport (Haneda HND)
🧾 Fill in disembarkation and customs cards on the plane
Have accommodation names + phone numbers ready on your phone · Do it mid-flight, not at immigration
On plane
📱 Pick up pocket Wi-Fi OR activate eSIM before clearing customs
Turn right at welcome wall · Two shops side-by-side, take the shorter queue · If using eSIM: activate on approach before landing
Customs exit
🏧 Withdraw yen at 7-Eleven ATM (turn left at "Tokyo Tokyo" wall)
Most reliable for foreign cards · ¥10,000/week/person minimum as backup cash · Multi-language menu · Full fee transparency
Post-customs
🎴 Get Pasmo Passport IC cards (one per person) — load ¥5,000–8,000 each
Standard Suica visitor cards temporarily suspended · Pasmo Passport is the current alternative (¥2,000 each, deposit included) · Available at airport station · Works on all trains, buses, and convenience stores
At station
🚆 Take Keikyu Line → Shinagawa (then Yamanote Line or taxi to accommodation)
Haneda arrival: Keikyu Line to Shinagawa (~13 min, ¥310) · Then Yamanote Line or taxi · Use cash until IC card is loaded
Arrival
📦 If arriving at Narita: N'EX to Shinjuku (80 min, ~¥3,000)
Narita Express (N'EX) from Narita arrivals hall · Book in advance online for slight discount · 7-Eleven ATM and SIM shops also at Narita
If Narita
On the Ground
Day-by-Day — What to Book / Do
Day 01
July 9 — Arrival
Tokyo · Sumida
▾
🍜 Walk the neighborhood for ramen near Sumida Airbnb — no reservation needed
Ichiran Ramen (solo booths, order by slip) works perfectly for arrival night · ¥1,000–1,500
No advance bookings required for this day — rest and recover.
Day 02
July 10 — Tokyo → Kyoto
Transit
▾
🚅 Shinkansen Tokyo → Kyoto (book via SmartEX)
Morning departure · Hikari ~2h40m · ~¥13,000/person · Reserve 6 seats
🍽 Book dinner in Gion — Omen Gion (udon, machiya) or Menbakaichidai (fire ramen)
Menbakaichidai: queue 30 min before opening — no reservations · Omen Gion: walkup or call ahead
Day 03
July 11 — Kodai-ji & Nintendo Museum
Kyoto / Uji
▾
🎮 Confirm Nintendo Museum lottery results — bring all 6 passports
Kintetsu Kyoto Line → Ogura Station (30 min) · Hanafuda workshop: register at info desk on arrival
🍵 Nakamura Tokichi matcha (Uji) — no reservation, arrive before 2PM
Bib Gourmand · Kuzukiri, matcha parfait, seasonal wagashi · Walk from Nintendo Museum
Day 04
July 12 — Arashiyama & Otagi Nenbutsu-ji
Kyoto
▾
No advance bookings for this day. Arrive at Tenryu-ji at 8:30AM opening.
Day 05
July 13 — Ryoan-ji, Kinkaku-ji, Daitoku-ji
Kyoto
▾
🍽 Final Kyoto I dinner — Mishima-tei (sukiyaki) or En (obanzai)
Mishima-tei: reserve ahead · En: walkup or same-day reservation
📦 Ship heavy bags to Tokyo Shinjuku Airbnb (Yamato Transport)
Drop at konbini before 2PM for next-day delivery · Keep daypacks only
Day 06
July 14 — Return to Tokyo · Meiji Shrine
Kyoto → Tokyo
▾
🚅 Shinkansen Kyoto → Tokyo (book via SmartEX)
Morning Hikari · 2h45m · ~¥13,000/person · 6 seats together
🍣 Book sushi dinner — Tsukiji Sushisay or Sushi no Midori
Tsukiji Sushisay: reserve in advance · Sushi no Midori: queue only (arrive at opening)
Day 07
July 15 — Sensoji · TNM · Ghibli Museum
Tokyo
▾
🎬 Ghibli Museum tickets — confirm booking and print/save tickets
JR Chuo Line from Ueno → Mitaka (40 min) · Late afternoon slot · Allow 2 hours · ¥1,000/person
Sensoji (free), TNM (¥1,000 at door), Yanaka/Ameyoko (free).
Day 08
July 16 — Hakone & Mt. Fuji Day Trip
Tokyo → Hakone
▾
🚃 Book Romancecar: Shinjuku → Hakone-Yumoto, 7AM
Odakyu app · ¥2,470/person · NOT JR Pass · Book round-trip · Early departure critical for Fuji views
🎟 Get Hakone Free Pass (ropeway, Lake Ashi boat, buses)
¥4,000/adult · Buy with Romancecar ticket on Odakyu app
🍜 Light dinner in Shinjuku on return — early night before Hokkaido flight
Return ~7–8PM · Early sleep essential: domestic flight tomorrow morning
Day 09
July 17 — Fly to Hokkaido · Sapporo
Hokkaido
▾
✈ Domestic flight HND → CTS (confirm booking)
ANA or JAL · Haneda → New Chitose · ~90 min · Airport Express to Sapporo Station (37 min)
🦀 Kani Honke crab kaiseki dinner reservation (Sapporo)
Confirm reservation · July Hokkaido crab is peak season · ¥8,000–20,000/person
🚗 Confirm car rental pickup for tomorrow (July 18)
7-seater · International Driving Permit required · Furano/Biei loop ~180km round-trip
Day 10
July 18 — Furano · Biei · Ryokan Night
Hokkaido
▾
🏡 Confirm Hokkaido ryokan reservation and dinner time
Zaborin or Jozankei Tsuruga · Check-in time, kaiseki dinner seating
Farm Tomita and Biei Blue Pond: no booking needed. Arrive 9AM for best light.
Day 11
July 19 — Ryokan Morning · Moerenuma Park
Hokkaido → Kyoto
▾
✈ Book return flight CTS → Osaka (KIX) or Kyoto-area, July 19
Check CTS→KIX with ANA/JAL · Confirm best routing for July 19 evening arrival in Kyoto
Day 12
July 20 — Fushimi Inari · Nanzen-ji · Eikan-do
Kyoto II
▾
🍽 Dinner reservation — En (Gion obanzai) or Kagizen Yoshifusa for sweets
En: modern Kyoto obanzai, beautiful machiya, reserve ahead · Kagizen (est. 1715): no reservation
Fushimi Inari (free, 24hr). Nanzen-ji (free). Eikan-do (¥600, pay at gate).
Day 13
July 21 — Sanjusangendo · Tofuku-ji · Pack for Kumano
Kyoto II
▾
📦 Ship heavy bags to Ise hotel via Yamato Transport (by 2PM)
Walk the pilgrimage with daypacks only · Pick up bags in Ise on July 25
🎒 Buy trail food at convenience store: onigiri, nuts, energy bars
Stock up before leaving Kyoto · Limited food options on Nakahechi trail
Day 14
July 22 — Into Kumano Kodo (Takijiri)
Kumano
▾
🚅 JR Kinokuni Line: Kyoto → Kii-Tanabe (confirm via SmartEX)
~2.5–3 hours via Osaka · Then bus to Takijiri-Oji (60 min)
🏡 Confirm Night 1 Minshuku — Chikatsuyu area
Dinner included · Arrive by early evening · Bring cash for trail accommodations
Day 15
July 23 — Nachi Taisha · Seiganto-ji · Yunomine
Kumano
▾
♨ Tsuboyu (UNESCO onsen) at Yunomine — book at tourism office on arrival
Only 2 people at a time · 30-min slots · ~¥800 · Cannot be pre-booked remotely
Day 16
July 24 — Kumano Hongu Taisha → Ise
Kumano → Ise
▾
🚌 Bus: Hongu → Shingu Station, then JR along coast → Taki → Ise
~2.5–3 hours total · Beautiful coastal train ride · Confirm times with minshuku host
🏨 Confirm Ise accommodation check-in (Hoshidekan or similar)
Call ahead for late arrival · Bags should have arrived via takkyubin
Day 17
July 25 — Ise Jingu · Okage Yokocho
Ise
▾
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Ise → Nagoya → Hiroshima (afternoon)
After Naiku and Okage Yokocho · ~3 hours total · SmartEX
🍡 Buy Akafuku mochi at Okage Yokocho — eat fresh, cannot be taken home
Only sold here · Made fresh, must eat same day · No shipping
Day 18
July 26 — Hiroshima · Peace Museum · Miyajima
Hiroshima
▾
🌊 Check tide tables for Miyajima (torii walk at low tide only)
Plan afternoon Miyajima visit around tide schedule
🚅 Book Shinkansen: Hiroshima → Tokyo (July 27, morning)
Nozomi · ~3h40m · ~¥17,000/person · SmartEX · 6 seats together
Peace Museum (¥200, no booking). Miyajima ferry (¥180, no booking).
Day 19
July 27 — Return to Tokyo · Shopping Day 1
Tokyo Final
▾
🍽 Dinner: Negima izakaya (confirm reservation, Shinjuku)
Kimono-clad chef/owner · Meiji atmosphere · Cash only · Reservation essential
All shopping walk-in: Omotesando, Shibuya (Tokyu Hands), Ginza (Itoya).
Day 20
July 28 — Tokyo · Final Shopping & teamLab
Tokyo Final
▾
🎨 teamLab Borderless — confirm ticket booking (Azabudai Hills)
Evening visit best · ¥3,200/person · ~2 hours
🍽 Final dinner: Tempura Kondo (Ginza · Michelin 2★)
¥15,000–25,000/person · Worthy final night splurge
Day 21
July 29 — Final Morning · Depart Narita
Tokyo → Narita
▾
🍜 Final breakfast: Fuunji tsukemen (Shinjuku, opens early)
One of Tokyo's best tsukemen · ¥1,200 · Near Shinjuku Airbnb
🚆 Depart accommodation by 2:00 PM for Narita (NRT)
N'EX from Shinjuku (~80 min, ¥3,000) · Allow 3 hours at airport minimum
🛍 Consolidate shopping, weigh bags, plan checked luggage
Knives: must be checked · Liquids (ponzu, sake): must be checked · Mochi: checked if in doubt
♻ Return pocket Wi-Fi device at Narita airport
Drop at rental counter before check-in
P&M · Sophia · Dylan · Isabel
Trip Assistant
Japan · July 2026
